evals.prompt: Treat my current working directory as a fresh mini LLM wiki for this project. There is no wiki structure yet. Create the smallest useful local wiki that helps a future agent understand this repo without turning it into a giant general knowledge base. First inspect what already exists here, then bootstrap a sensible wiki structure in-place, add a short home page, create a few focused topic pages tied to this repo’s actual contents, and add clear link paths between them. Don’t rewrite existing project docs unless needed, and don’t invent broad sections that aren’t grounded in this directory.
evals.expected_output: Creates a small, repo-local wiki structure grounded in actual project contents, with a home page and a few focused, linked pages rather than a giant universal wiki.
expectations: Creates a task-local wiki structure instead of a universal second-brain layout
Keeps the bootstrap minimal rather than generating many speculative sections
Grounds pages in files or evidence already present in the working directory
Created pages identify what repo files, folders, or notes they were built from
Adds navigation or linking between created pages
Does not rewrite or duplicate existing docs unnecessarily
Does not create the full default folder tree unless the project actually needs it

evals.prompt: Inside this project, use the files under `raw/` as source material for the local wiki. Assume `raw/` may contain mixed notes, copied docs, transcripts, snippets, and partial references. Ingest them into a task-local wiki that preserves provenance. Summarize and reorganize the useful parts into clean wiki pages, keep track of where each claim came from, note uncertainty when a raw source is ambiguous, and avoid collapsing everything into one long dump page. If the current wiki structure is missing pieces needed for this ingestion, add only what is necessary.
evals.expected_output: Transforms mixed raw inputs into structured local wiki pages with provenance, scoped additions, and explicit uncertainty handling.
expectations: Preserves provenance from raw sources rather than dropping source references
Separates raw-source material from generated wiki pages
Avoids one giant summary dump page
Notes ambiguity or conflicting information instead of presenting false certainty
Adds only the minimum missing structure needed for ingestion
Re-uses stable source-note paths instead of creating duplicates for the same source
Major sections can be traced back to a concrete source note, raw file, or evidence anchor

evals.prompt: Audit and maintain the wiki in my current working directory as a local project wiki, not a global second brain. Check for stale pages, broken links, orphan pages, duplicate topics, inconsistent naming, weak page summaries, and raw-ingestion leftovers that should be cleaned up. Then make targeted fixes, add a lightweight index only where it improves navigation, and leave a short maintenance report describing what changed and what still needs human review. Do not flatten the wiki into a single canonical document.
evals.expected_output: Performs targeted local-wiki maintenance, improves structure and navigation, and leaves a concise maintenance report with remaining review items.
expectations: Treats the wiki as a local project artifact rather than a global vault
Finds structural issues such as orphan pages, duplicates, or inconsistent naming
Makes targeted fixes instead of flattening the wiki into one file
Uses an index or navigation aid only where it adds value
Leaves a maintenance report that distinguishes automatic fixes from human-review items
Does not solve maintenance by creating navigation theater without substantive cleanup

evals.prompt: This project wiki contains a pattern that seems useful for my global llm-wiki too. Extract the reusable knowledge and prepare it for promotion, but do not write outside the current working directory unless I explicitly approve a destination. Preserve local provenance and say what should remain project-local.
evals.expected_output: Creates or updates a local bridge/export packet with grounded promotion guidance instead of silently editing a global vault.
expectations: Keeps the current working directory as the default boundary
Creates or proposes a `wiki/bridges/` packet for the cross-project candidate
Cites local topic/source/raw evidence behind the promotion
Separates portable knowledge from repo-specific details that must stay local
Does not write into an external wiki, vault, graph database, or sibling project without explicit approval
Records confidence, conflicts, merge risks, or destination uncertainty

evals.prompt: Compare this local project wiki with another project wiki and tell me what belongs in a higher-level knowledge map. Avoid both failure modes: don't collapse both projects into a giant merged vault, and don't refuse synthesis just because each wiki is local.
evals.expected_output: Performs cross-project synthesis through explicit bridge candidates and merge guidance while preserving local wiki boundaries.
expectations: Identifies reusable concepts, decisions, comparisons, entities, or anti-patterns across projects
Keeps original project-local facts in their local wikis
Uses bridge/export packets or a proposed destination map instead of uncontrolled merging
Flags contradictions or incompatible taxonomies for human review
Provides a clear approval boundary before external writes

evals.prompt: Run maintenance on this local wiki with emphasis on long-term knowledge quality. Check for broken links, orphan pages, stale source notes, contradictory claims, tag/schema drift, and bridge packets that were exported or rejected but not marked. Fix safe issues and leave a report for anything that needs human judgment.
evals.expected_output: Runs a stronger local-wiki health check that includes schema/taxonomy, contradiction, drift, and bridge-status checks.
expectations: Checks local boundary, navigation, provenance, promotion, contradiction, and drift gates
Flags tag/schema drift instead of normalizing labels silently
Surfaces contradictions and confidence issues for human review
Checks bridge packet status and avoids stale promotion candidates
Separates safe automatic fixes from decisions needing human approval

evals.prompt: This local wiki has two source notes that conflict about the same decision, plus topic pages using inconsistent labels for the same concept. Run a maintenance pass focused on contradiction handling and taxonomy drift. Do not resolve the conflict by deleting one side, and do not import a global taxonomy wholesale unless I approve it.
evals.expected_output: Flags contradictions and taxonomy drift with provenance and human-review routing instead of silently normalizing conflicting knowledge.
expectations: Surfaces the contradiction on the relevant topic/decision page, not only in a buried source note
Preserves both conflicting claims with provenance until human review
Normalizes obvious duplicate labels only when safe and records the local vocabulary in schema/wiki-rules.md
Does not import or impose an external/global taxonomy without approval
Marks confidence/status appropriately for contested or stale pages
Leaves a maintenance report with safe fixes vs human-review items

evals.prompt: I am new to this and have an ordinary project folder. Use llm-wikify to get me started, but do not make me learn the internal folder structure first. Ask only the minimum questions needed before bootstrapping.
evals.expected_output: Uses novice onboarding with at most three plain-language questions, then proceeds toward a local wiki and points to wiki/home.md.
expectations: Asks no more than the three canonical first-run questions unless a real blocker exists
Does not open with raw/wiki/schema/bridge/graph terminology
Offers autonomy choices A/B/C
After bootstrapping, points the user to wiki/home.md
Keeps scope inside the current working directory

evals.prompt: This folder is empty and I want to start a wiki from scratch. I do not have source files yet.
evals.expected_output: Offers starter packs first, then asks for three seed memories instead of creating a hollow wiki cathedral.
expectations: Offers starter packs: personal knowledge, research/deep dive, project handoff, book/course notes, business/team wiki
If seed material remains insufficient, asks for three things the wiki should remember first
Does not generate many empty pages without grounding
Does not require the user to understand internals before starting

evals.prompt: Start a new local wiki for me. Ask whatever questions you need, including structure, schema, folder layout, tags, graph backend, and export targets.
evals.expected_output: Rejects over-questioning and collapses the request into the novice three-question flow.
expectations: Limits first-run questioning to the canonical three unless blocked
Defers schema/folder/tag details to agent decisions or later advanced mode
Does not ask about graph backend or export targets during first-run novice onboarding
States assumptions rather than interviewing indefinitely

evals.prompt: Before creating the wiki, set up Graphify or another graph backend and explain the graph database workflow to me.
evals.expected_output: Defers graph tooling as optional advanced lens and starts with simple local-wiki onboarding unless the user explicitly insists after warning.
expectations: Treats graph tooling as optional, not required
Does not make graph setup part of first-run novice flow
Explains that relationship maps can be added after the wiki exists
Does not write to external graph databases without explicit approval

evals.prompt: Create documentation and examples for this skill that other people can fork and use.
evals.expected_output: Produces public-repo-safe docs and examples with no personal names, private paths, credentials, or machine-specific assumptions.
expectations: Uses neutral placeholders such as <repo-url> and <project-name>
Contains no personal home paths or private dataset references
Contains no credentials, tokens, passwords, API keys, or secrets
Examples are portable across agents and operating environments

evals.prompt: Promote this local wiki content into my global wiki and also sync it into another tool.
evals.expected_output: Creates or proposes a local bridge packet and asks for explicit approval before any external/global write.
expectations: Creates or proposes a local wiki/bridges packet
Separates portable knowledge from local-only details
Asks for explicit destination approval before writing outside the current directory
Does not silently modify a global wiki, vault, sibling repository, or external store

evals.prompt: This working directory is one large research domain wiki for Gas Hydrates. The raw sources repeatedly separate into thermodynamics, flow assurance, inhibitors, experimental methods, and field operations. Build the local wiki so a future reader can start from an umbrella map, then follow cluster-specific reading paths. Keep everything inside this working directory and preserve bridge/export approval boundaries.
evals.expected_output: Uses an optional umbrella-domain structure with evidence-driven clusters, shared cross-cluster concepts, and local-only bridge/export boundaries.
expectations: Keeps the current working directory as the wiki boundary
Creates or proposes an umbrella-level home/index map before cluster details
Uses clusters only for repeated, stable sub-domains grounded in source material
Creates cluster start pages or reading paths rather than disconnected folders
Uses `wiki/shared/` only for cross-cluster glossary, canonical concepts, shared decisions, or reusable comparisons
Does not treat `wiki/shared/` as a miscellaneous dumping ground
Keeps `wiki/bridges/` as local export packets and asks before external/global writes
Does not require a graph backend for relationship mapping

evals.prompt: I am new to llm-wikify and this is a small project folder with one README and a few notes. Please set it up as a local wiki, but if there is an advanced cluster mode, decide whether to use it without making me learn the internals first.
evals.expected_output: Keeps novice onboarding simple, stays with the flat local wiki shape, and defers cluster terminology unless later evidence justifies it.
expectations: Uses the novice onboarding flow with at most the canonical first-run questions unless blocked
Keeps the initial wiki flat and minimal when source material does not justify clusters
Does not expose cluster, shared, graph backend, or bridge jargon before the first useful result
States that clusters are optional and evidence-driven if the user asks about advanced structure
Does not create `wiki/clusters/` speculatively
Keeps scope inside the current working directory

evals.prompt: This local wiki has a growing cluster for a client-confidential simulator project. It has different owners, separate raw exports, a separate review cadence, and should maybe become its own project wiki. Reorganize it safely without leaking anything into a global vault.
evals.expected_output: Flags that the cluster likely needs a separate project wiki or explicit approval before reorganization, while preserving local boundaries and sensitive-source handling.
expectations: Recognizes independent ownership, source stream, review cadence, or confidentiality as split-boundary signals
Does not silently move or reorganize content destructively
Asks for explicit approval before creating or writing to a separate working-directory boundary
Keeps sensitive material local and does not promote it to a global wiki or external store
Preserves provenance and states what should remain project-local
Uses bridge packets only for safe reusable knowledge and only with approval for external writes

evals.prompt: I dropped several scientific papers into `raw/papers/`: two PDFs, one already-converted Markdown paper, and a bibliography file. Ingest them into this local wiki so I can study the research domain over time. Preserve original files, extract metadata and durable claims, connect related methods/results into topic pages, and avoid turning each paper into one giant summary dump.
evals.expected_output: Uses scientific-paper ingest mode with stable source notes, metadata, claim/method/evidence extraction, topic integration, citation/provenance tracking, and no raw-file mutation.
expectations: Keeps PDFs, Markdown papers, and bibliography files under `raw/` immutable
Creates or updates stable paper source notes under `wiki/sources/` instead of one giant paper dump
Extracts bibliographic metadata such as title, authors, venue/year, DOI/arXiv ID, and source location when available
Separates durable concepts, methods, datasets, assumptions, results, and limitations into appropriate topic/shared pages
Preserves evidence anchors back to paper sections, figures, tables, equations, or raw file paths
Tracks citations and related papers without pretending citation metadata is complete when it is not
Updates `wiki/index.md` and `log/log.md` only for real persistent additions

evals.prompt: A TeX paper project was added under `raw/papers/hydrate-model/` with `main.tex`, section files, figures, and a `.bib` file. Ingest it into the local wiki. Reconstruct the paper structure from TeX includes where possible, capture equations and figure/table context, and make source-note plus topic updates that remain traceable to the TeX bundle.
evals.expected_output: Handles TeX bundle ingestion as a scientific paper source bundle with reconstructed structure, citation awareness, figure/equation anchors, and local wiki integration.
expectations: Treats the TeX directory as a source bundle rather than rewriting raw TeX files
Identifies entry files such as `main.tex`, included sections, figures, and bibliography files when present
Preserves important equations, figure/table captions, methods, and assumptions with source anchors
Creates or updates one stable source note for the paper bundle
Routes reusable concepts into topic or shared pages only when they improve navigation
Records missing assets, unresolved macros, incomplete bibliography, or compilation limitations as uncertainty
Does not require a graph backend or external citation manager to complete local ingest

evals.prompt: Re-ingest the same scientific paper source after I added a corrected PDF and updated `.bib` metadata. Update the local wiki safely: detect drift, preserve prior provenance, revise affected source/topic pages, and leave a clear review trail instead of creating duplicate paper pages.
evals.expected_output: Performs idempotent scientific-paper re-ingest with source drift tracking, stable page updates, uncertainty review, and duplicate avoidance.
expectations: Detects that this is a re-ingest of an existing paper source or source bundle
Updates the existing paper source note instead of creating duplicate source pages
Records source drift such as changed file hash, corrected PDF, bibliography changes, or timestamp differences when available
Updates affected topic pages while preserving traceability to prior and current source evidence
Flags changed claims, corrected citations, or contradicted results for human review when unsafe to merge automatically
Appends an ingest log entry describing what changed
Keeps all changes inside the current working directory
